info.name: LunchBox AI
info.description:
Complete API collection for LunchBox AI backend.

Base URL: http://localhost:5100

Collection variables:
- `base_url` — server origin (default: http://localhost:5100)
- `token`    — JWT; auto-populated by Register / Login test scripts
- `child_id` — auto-populated by Add Child test script
- `session_id` — auto-populated by Create Session test script
- `food_item_id` — auto-populated by Create Food Item test script
- `nutrition_goal_id` — auto-populated by Create Nutrition Goal test script
- `allergen_id` — master-data allergen ID (1=Peanuts, 2=Tree Nuts, 3=Milk/Dairy, 4=Eggs, 5=Wheat/Gluten, 6=Soy, 7=Fish, 8=Shellfish, 9=Sesame, 10=Mustard, …15); set manually before using child-allergen endpoints

Run order for a full E2E flow:
1. Register (or Login)
2. List Allergens → pick allergen IDs
3. List School Rules → pick rule IDs
4. List Avatars → pick avatar ID
5. Add Child
6. Create Session (generate lunchbox)
7. Get Session History

variable.description: Master-data allergen ID. 1=Peanuts, 2=Tree Nuts, 3=Milk/Dairy, 4=Eggs, 5=Wheat/Gluten, 6=Soy, 7=Fish, 8=Shellfish, 9=Sesame, 10=Mustard, 11=Celery, 12=Lupin, 13=Sulphites, 14=Corn, 15=Latex-Fruit. Set manually before calling child-allergen endpoints.

request.description: Verify the server is running and connected to MySQL. Returns `{ status: 'ok', timestamp }`. No auth required.

Register a new parent account.

Required fields:
- `email` — valid email address
- `password` — min 6 characters
- `full_name` — 2–100 characters

Returns `{ data: { token, user } }`. Token is auto-saved to the `token` collection variable.

HTTP 409 if email already registered.

Login with email + password.

Returns `{ data: { token, user } }`. Token is auto-saved to the `token` collection variable.

HTTP 401 if credentials are invalid.

Returns the authenticated user's profile: `{ data: { user } }`.

Requires Bearer token.

Update the authenticated user's display name.

Required: `full_name` (string).

Returns updated user object.

Initiates Google OAuth2 flow. Must be opened in a browser — not testable directly in Postman.

On success, redirects to `{FRONTEND_URL}/auth/callback?token=<jwt>`.

Initiates Facebook OAuth flow. Must be opened in a browser — not testable directly in Postman.

On success, redirects to `{FRONTEND_URL}/auth/callback?token=<jwt>`.

Returns the master list of 15 seeded allergens (Big-9 + others).

Public — no auth required.

Response: `{ data: { allergens: [ { id, name, icon, category, description, is_common } ] } }`

Use `id` values when building a child profile (allergen_ids / allergens[].allergen_id).

Returns the master list of 8 seeded school rules.

Public — no auth required.

Response: `{ data: { school_rules: [ { id, name, description, is_active } ] } }`

Use `id` values in `school_rule_ids` when creating or updating a child profile.

query.description: Set to true to include inactive food items (admin use)

request.description:
Returns all active food items.

Public — no auth required.

Response: `{ data: { food_items: [ { id, name, category, is_active, created_at, updated_at } ] } }`
Returns all food items including inactive ones. Public endpoint (auth optional).

Create a new food item. Requires auth.

Required: `name` (1–100 chars).
Optional: `category` (max 50 chars), `is_active` (boolean, default true).

HTTP 409 if a food item with that name already exists.

Update an existing food item by ID. Requires auth.

All fields optional: `name`, `category`, `is_active`.

HTTP 404 if not found. HTTP 409 on duplicate name.
Soft-deactivate a food item (sets is_active = false). Requires auth.

Permanently delete a food item by ID. Requires auth.

HTTP 204 on success. HTTP 404 if not found.

query.description: Set to true to include inactive goals

request.description:
Returns all active nutrition goals.

Public — no auth required.

Response: `{ data: { nutrition_goals: [ { id, goal_key, label, description, is_active } ] } }`

Seeded keys: balanced, high_protein, high_calories, low_sugar, low_carb, high_fiber.
Returns all nutrition goals including inactive. Public endpoint (auth optional).

Create a new nutrition goal. Requires auth.

Required: `goal_key` (1–50 chars, unique), `label` (1–100 chars).
Optional: `description`, `is_active`.

HTTP 409 if goal_key already exists.

Update a nutrition goal by ID. Requires auth.

All fields optional: `goal_key`, `label`, `description`, `is_active`.

HTTP 404 if not found. HTTP 409 on duplicate goal_key.

Permanently delete a nutrition goal by ID. Requires auth.

HTTP 204 on success. HTTP 404 if not found.

Returns all active avatars. Requires auth.

Response: `{ data: { avatars: [ { id, name, filename, image_data, is_active, created_at } ] } }`

Each avatar includes `image_data` as a base64 data-URI (e.g. `data:image/png;base64,...`).
Use directly as `<Image source={{ uri: item.image_data }} />` in React Native.

Seeded: Lion(1), Panda(2), Fox(3), Bunny(4), Bear(5), Cat(6), Dog(7), Penguin(8).
Use `id` as `avatar_id` when creating or updating a child profile.

Create a child profile. Requires auth. Scoped to the authenticated user.

Required: `name` (1–100 chars).
Optional:
- `date_of_birth` — ISO date YYYY-MM-DD, must be in the past
- `avatar_id` — from GET /api/avatars
- `allergen_ids` — array of allergen IDs (all assigned with severity=allergy)
- `allergens` — richer format: `[{ allergen_id, severity, notes }]` (see Add Child (detailed allergens) example)
- `school_rule_ids` — array of school rule IDs from GET /api/school-rules

Child ID is auto-saved to `child_id` variable.

Response includes child with nested `allergens`, `school_rules`, and `avatar`.

Create a child profile using the richer `allergens` array format which allows specifying per-allergen `severity` and `notes`.

`severity` values: `intolerance` | `allergy` | `severe`

If both `allergens` and `allergen_ids` are provided, the `allergens` array takes precedence.

Returns all children belonging to the authenticated user.

Each child includes nested `allergens`, `school_rules`, and `avatar` objects.

Update a child profile. Requires auth. Must own the child.

All fields optional: `name`, `date_of_birth`, `avatar_id` (pass null to clear), `allergen_ids`, `allergens`, `school_rule_ids`.

Note: `allergen_ids` / `allergens` fully replaces the child's current allergens (pass empty array `[]` to clear all). Use `allergens` for the richer format with severity + notes.

Note: `school_rule_ids` fully replaces the child's current rules (pass empty array `[]` to clear all rules).

HTTP 404 if child not found or not owned by caller.

Delete a child profile and cascade-delete all related allergens, school rules, and lunchbox sessions. Requires auth. Must own the child.

HTTP 204 on success.

Add or update an allergen on a child profile. Requires auth.

Required: `allergen_id` (integer, from GET /api/allergens).
Optional: `severity` (`intolerance` | `allergy` | `severe`, default `allergy`), `notes` (max 255 chars).

If the allergen already exists on the child, its severity and notes are updated (upsert).

Returns the full allergen list for the child.

HTTP 404 if child or allergen not found.

Remove a specific allergen from a child profile. Requires auth.

HTTP 204 on success. HTTP 404 if child not found or not owned by caller.

formdata.description: REQUIRED — photo of the empty lunchbox (JPEG / PNG / WebP, max 10 MB)
Optional — ingredient photos available in the fridge (up to 5 files, JPEG / PNG / WebP)
Optional — child profile ID. If provided, child's allergens, school rules, and nutrition goals are included in the AI prompt.

formdata.value: Please make it colourful and fun!
formdata.description: Optional — free-text parent notes passed to the AI
Optional — maximum preparation time in minutes (tinyint, 0–255)
Optional — override the child's default nutrition goal for this session. Use a goal_key value (e.g. 'high_protein', 'low_sugar')
Optional — free-text list of foods the child dislikes for this session (overrides child profile)
Optional — free-text school rule notes for this session (overrides child profile rules)
Optional — JSON array of extra allergen IDs to add for this session only. Example: [1,3,5]. These are stored in session_allergen_overrides and merged with child allergens during AI analysis.

request.description:
Upload a lunchbox image (and optional ingredient photos) to trigger the full AI pipeline.

Pipeline steps:
1. GPT-4o vision analyzes the lunchbox shape, compartments, and available ingredients
2. GPT-image-1 generates a filled lunchbox image
3. Result is persisted to `lunchbox_results` and session status set to `completed`

Runtime: ~25–60 seconds (synchronous).

Response fields:
- `session` — full session record
- `filledLunchboxUrl` — base64 data-URI of the generated lunchbox image
- `foodItems` — array of suggested food items
- `lunchboxDescription` — AI description of the lunchbox
- `compartmentCount` — detected number of compartments
- `detectedShape` — e.g. 'rectangular', 'round'
- `detectedOrientation` — e.g. 'landscape', 'portrait'
- `generatedImageAnalysis` — analysis of the generated image
- `generationAttempts` — array of attempt summaries
- `processingMs` — total pipeline time in milliseconds

HTTP 400 if lunchbox image is missing. HTTP 404 if child_id not found.

Returns paginated lunchbox session history for the authenticated user.

Response: `{ data: { sessions, total, page, limit, totalPages } }`

Use `child_id` query param to filter sessions by a specific child.
Same as Get Session History but filtered to a specific child.

Returns a single lunchbox session by ID.

Includes the full AI result:
- `ai_text_response` — AI description
- `suggested_items` — JSON array of food items
- `generated_image_b64` — base64 data-URI of the generated lunchbox image
- `processing_ms`, `ai_model`, `tokens_used`

Also includes ingredient images and session allergen overrides.

HTTP 404 if session not found or not owned by caller.

Delete a lunchbox session by ID. Requires auth. Must own the session.

Also deletes all associated files from disk (lunchbox image, ingredient images).

HTTP 204 on success. HTTP 404 if session not found.
